content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Opinion Request Meeting Participants  
Date: Jan. 27, 1982

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Alice

Subject:

The following questions were recent phone inquiries. Janis has not yet had an opportunity to look into the advice files to see if we've given any relevant past advice, but, in the meantime, we thought we would ask for your thoughts on the questions.

1. A staff member of the Republican Caucus phoned to ask whether Gala tickets received in December 1981, but not used until January 4, 1982, must be reported in a 1981 or 1982 Statement of Economic Interests.

The staff member indicated that the filer he was calling for was willing to report the tickets on his 1981 Statement. However, I am sure that we will be receiving additional questions on this from filers who will prefer not to report the tickets this year because of the upcoming election. If re-elected, such officials would not report the tickets until March 1, 1983.

Which Statement should the Gala tickets be reported on; the one filed March 1, 1982, or March 1, 1983?

2. Lou Brizzolara of the Orange County Superior Court asked the following question:

A Judge sold his property and a person assumed his loan. If the buyer defaults on the loan, the Judge will have to reassume the loan and repay it. Is the real property reportable on his Statement of Economic Interests?

JSM:km

sections.question_synthetic: This is an internal FPPC memorandum presenting two phone inquiry questions for staff discussion: (1) whether Gala tickets received in December 1981 but used in January 1982 should be reported on the 1981 or 1982 Statement of Economic Interests, and (2) whether a judge's real property remains reportable on their Statement of Economic Interests when the buyer has assumed the loan but may default.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal staff memorandum, not a formal advice letter response. It presents questions for internal discussion rather than providing FPPC advice or conclusions. No section headers for QUESTION or CONCLUSION exist. Document appears to be preliminary in nature, noting that files have not yet been reviewed for relevant past advice.
